refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and site-specific duty cycle context, making the requirement verifiable and traceable to operational constraints.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct field trials under representative duty cycle conditions; collect cycle time data across ≥100 cycles; compute p95 percentile; compare against CT_target; validate telemetry reporting accuracy.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within individual time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote cycle execution with latency and control responsiveness that does not degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce operational constraints (e.g., speed limits, geofencing, emergency stops) without causing cycle time to exceed CT_target under normal operation.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time telemetry to enable performance tracking and root-cause analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle powertrain efficiency, hydraulic response time, and load handling speed; implement predictive cycle planning to minimize idle time.
DP-B: Implement low-latency teleoperation network with edge computing; optimize control algorithms for minimal operator-induced delays.
DP-C: Design safety constraint enforcement as non-blocking advisory overlays rather than hard stops; use predictive geofencing to pre-warn operators.
DP-D: Deploy real-time cycle time telemetry collection with edge aggregation; expose p95 metrics via standardized API for customer dashboards.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-Integrated: Combine optimized vehicle performance (DP-A) with low-latency teleoperation (DP-B), non-blocking safety overlays (DP-C), and real-time telemetry (DP-D) as an integrated system design that balances productivity, safety, and observability.

potential_sources: Safety constraint enforcement (DP-C) may couple with vehicle speed optimization (DP-A) if geofencing or speed limits are site-specific; mitigation: parameterize safety constraints per site.
Teleoperation latency (DP-B) couples with cycle time budget (DP-A); mitigation: establish network SLA and validate end-to-end latency in acceptance testing.
Telemetry collection (DP-D) may introduce minor computational overhead on vehicle controller; mitigation: use asynchronous, non-blocking telemetry writes.
coupling_check.notes: The integrated DP is loosely coupled if each sub-component (vehicle, teleoperation, safety, telemetry) maintains clear interfaces and parameterization. Coupling is manageable through interface contracts and site-specific configuration.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic and hydraulic limits; Risk: If target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy it—requires early validation with customer.
Assumption: Network infrastructure at customer site supports low-latency teleoperation; Risk: Poor network conditions may violate DP-B; mitigation: conduct site network assessment pre-deployment.

Assumption: Safety constraints can be enforced as advisory overlays without regulatory violation; Risk: Some jurisdictions may require hard stops; mitigation: validate against worksite safety plan and local regulations.

Assumption: Telemetry collection does not introduce unacceptable latency or computational burden; Risk: Edge aggregation may fail under high-frequency sampling; mitigation: design telemetry with configurable sampling rates.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose DP-Integrated into four distinct sub-DPs (DP-A, DP-B, DP-C, DP-D) at the next level to enable independent design, testing, and ownership. Establish explicit interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) between sub-systems to manage coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system integration module that orchestrates vehicle performance, teleoperation, safety services, and telemetry collection to achieve the cycle time KPI. It owns the end-to-end cycle time performance budget and coordinates sub-system contributions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exports cycle time metrics (p95, mean, variance), vehicle state (position, load, speed), and safety events to customer dashboards and monitoring systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives cycle task definitions (load location, dump location, duty cycle parameters) from site manager or planning system.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (geofence violations, emergency stop triggers, constraint violations) to safety services and operator interfaces.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains bidirectional network connectivity for teleoperation commands, telemetry uplink, and safety alert distribution.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for cycle time KPI performance. All sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are logically contained within NGHE Solution's scope. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns powertrain and load handling performance (DP-A) but does not own teleoperation control logic (DP-B), safety constraint enforcement (DP-C), or telemetry aggregation (DP-D). Assigning FR-2 to Vehicle alone would diffuse cycle time responsibility across multiple modules.

Teleoperation owns network latency and control responsiveness (DP-B) but does not own vehicle performance (DP-A), safety enforcement (DP-C), or telemetry (DP-D). Assigning FR-2 to Teleoperation alone would fragment cycle time accountability.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement (DP-C) but does not own vehicle performance (DP-A), teleoperation (DP-B), or telemetry (DP-D). Assigning FR-2 to Safety Services would obscure the primary performance driver (vehicle and teleoperation).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it integrates all four sub-DPs (vehicle performance, teleoperation, safety, telemetry) and owns the end-to-end cycle time KPI. Sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and contribute to the KPI through well-defined interfaces.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration module; detailed cycle time performance may be diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules. Mitigation: Establish explicit cycle time budgets for each sub-system and track via Telemetry interface.

Risk: Cycle time performance depends on site-specific conditions (network latency, duty cycle, safety constraints) that may not be fully controllable by NGHE Solution. Mitigation: Parameterize NGHE Solution design to accommodate site variability; validate CT_target feasibility pre-deployment.

Risk: Telemetry collection and reporting (DP-D) may introduce latency or computational overhead that impacts cycle time. Mitigation: Design telemetry as non-blocking, asynchronous process; validate impact via performance testing.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: At the next decomposition level, assign sub-FRs (FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4) to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and a dedicated Telemetry/Monitoring module respectively. Establish explicit cycle time budgets and interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) to manage integration and accountability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target with telemetry monitoring) is directly addressed by the selected DP (integrated optimization of vehicle, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry). The DP is logically sound and feasible given the constraints. No fundamental mismatch between problem and solution.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time performance is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules. NGHE Solution owns integration but not all performance drivers. Mitigation: Establish clear cycle time budgets and ownership for each sub-system.
Telemetry collection (DP-D) is assigned to NGHE Solution but may be implemented by multiple sub-systems. Mitigation: Define telemetry interface contract and centralize aggregation logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for the integrated DP. Sub-systems are logically contained and communicate via known interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Containment score of 95 supports this assignment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via Telemetry interface. Field trials under representative duty cycle can validate p95 performance. Verification is feasible if CT_target is realistic and site conditions are representative.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-2 is allocated to NGHE Solution with clear sub-FRs (FR-2.1 to FR-2.4) for next-level decomposition. Ownership and accountability are clear at the system level; detailed allocation to sub-systems will be refined in next iteration.

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit cycle time budgets for Vehicle (FR-2.1), Teleoperation (FR-2.2), Safety Services (FR-2.3), and Telemetry (FR-2.4) to enable distributed accountability and performance tracking.
Define interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link to specify data formats, latency requirements, and error handling.
Conduct early feasibility study to validate that CT_target is achievable given vehicle kinematic limits, network latency, and safety constraints. If not feasible, revise CT_target or DP.
Validate that safety constraint enforcement (DP-C) can be implemented as non-blocking advisory overlays without violating regulatory or worksite safety plan requirements.

decision.justification: The refined FR-2 and selected DP are sound and well-aligned. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary with high containment (95%) and minimal diffusion (5%). However, the DP is integrated across four distinct sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry), and cycle time performance is distributed. To manage complexity and accountability, decomposition to the next level is necessary. Sub-FRs (FR-2.1 to FR-2.4) are well-defined and ready for independent design and ownership assignment.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within individual time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote cycle execution with latency and control responsiveness that does not degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce operational constraints without causing cycle time to exceed CT_target under normal operation.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time telemetry to enable performance tracking and root-cause analysis.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic and hydraulic limits.
Network infrastructure at customer site supports low-latency teleoperation (latency budget TBD).
Safety constraints can be enforced as advisory overlays without regulatory violation.
Telemetry collection does not introduce unacceptable latency or computational burden.
Site-specific duty cycle and conditions are representative of acceptance test scenarios.
p95 percentile is the agreed metric for cycle time performance (not mean or p99).
open_risks: CT_target feasibility: If target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy it. Early validation required.
Network latency: Poor site network conditions may violate teleoperation latency budget. Site assessment required pre-deployment.
Safety regulatory compliance: Advisory overlay approach may not comply with local regulations. Validation against worksite safety plan required.
Telemetry overhead: Edge aggregation may fail under high-frequency sampling or network congestion. Performance testing required.
Sub-system integration: Cycle time performance depends on coordinated optimization across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Integration testing required.
evidence_needed: CT_target feasibility study: Vehicle performance envelope, network latency budget, safety constraint impact analysis.
Site network assessment: Latency, bandwidth, reliability measurements at customer site.
Regulatory compliance review: Worksite safety plan, local regulations, advisory vs. hard-stop constraint enforcement.
Telemetry design specification: Sampling rates, aggregation logic, latency impact analysis.
Sub-system interface contracts: Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link data formats and latency requirements.
Field trial plan: Duty cycle definition, sample size (≥100 cycles), p95 calculation methodology, acceptance criteria.
